body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/` **Triggered by:** PR #57653 (commit fe4c0e1e) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed PR #57653 updated `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles` to automatically generate three scaffolding files when code is first emitted into a directory under `sdk/`: - `README.md` — pre-filled with standard section headers (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) - `CHANGELOG.md` — pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template - `Directory.Build.props` — pre-filled with the standard import chain for azure-sdk-for-net packages Files are only written if they do **not** already exist, so customised content is never overwritten. ## Gaps Found - The emitter README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` has no mention of these auto-generated files or when they are produced - The `new-project` option description only says it overwrites the `.csproj`; there is no explanation that new scaffolding files (README, CHANGELOG, Directory.Build.props) are also created on first use under `sdk/` - There is no note that the scaffolding is intentionally a one-shot operation (files are not overwritten on subsequent regenerations), which is important for users who need to know they can safely customise these files - No guidance on what the generated templates look like or how to extend them via the `GetReadmeContent` / `GetChangelogContent` / `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent` virtual methods for custom generators
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add a new sub-section under `## Emitter options` (after the `new-project` option entry) and a new top-level section for scaffolding behaviour. Add the following after the `### new-project` option block: ````markdown ### Scaffolded files (new projects under `sdk/`) When generating a **new** project whose output directory is located under the `sdk/` directory of the azure-sdk-for-net repository, the generator automatically creates the following files alongside the `.csproj` and `.sln` files — **only if they do not already exist**: | File | Description | |---|---| | `README.md` | Pre-filled with standard azure-sdk-for-net section headings (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) | | `CHANGELOG.md` | Pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template | | `Directory.Build.props` | Pre-filled with the standard MSBuild import chain required by azure-sdk-for-net | Because these files are written only once, you can freely customise them after they are generated — subsequent runs of the generator will never overwrite your changes. > **Note:** This scaffolding is only triggered when the emitter output directory contains `/sdk/` in its path. Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files. #### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator If you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content: ```csharp protected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... } protected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... } ``` ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included) No snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Azure Generator] Docs: Document auto-scaffolding of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props
